nodes.label: Paul R. Hill
nodes.summary: Paul Richard Hill (1909–1990) was a mid–twentieth-century American aerodynamicist. He was a leading research and development engineer and manager for NASA (National Aeronautics and Space Administration) and its predecessor, NACA (the National Advisory Council for Aeronautics) between 1939 and 1970,

nodes.label: International UFO Museum and Research Center
nodes.summary: The International UFO Museum and Research Center is located in Roswell, New Mexico, United States, in the downtown district, and is focused largely on the 1947 Roswell Crash and later supposed UFO incidents in the United States and elsewhere. nodes.label: The Report on Unidentified Flying Objects
nodes.summary: The Report on Unidentified Flying Objects is a 1956 book by then-retired Air Force UFO investigator Edward J. Ruppelt, detailing his experience running Project Blue Book. The book was noted for its suggestion that a few UFO sightings might be linked to spikes of atomic radiation. Contemporary media

nodes.label: Identification studies of UFOs
nodes.summary: Identifying unidentified flying objects (UFOs) is a difficult task due to the normally poor quality of the evidence provided by those who report sighting the unknown object. nodes.label: Flying Saucer Working Party

nodes.label: Unidentified flying object
nodes.summary: An unidentified flying object (UFO) is an object or phenomenon seen in the sky but not yet identified or explained. nodes.label: Roswell That Ends Well
nodes.summary: "Roswell That Ends Well" is the nineteenth episode in the third season of the American animated television series Futurama, and the 51st episode of the series overall. It originally aired on the Fox network in the United States on December 9, 2001. The plot centers on an accidental time travel event

nodes.label: The Galileo Project
nodes.summary: The Galileo Project is an international scientific research project to search for extraterrestrial intelligence or extraterrestrial technology on and near Earth and to identify the nature of anomalous Unidentified Flying Objects/Unidentified Aerial Phenomena (UFOs/UAP). nodes.label: World Contact Day
nodes.summary: World Contact Day was first declared in March 1953 by an organization called the International Flying Saucer Bureau (IFSB), as a day on which all IFSB members would attempt to send a telepathic message into space. nodes.label: Steven M. Greer
nodes.summary: Steven Macon Greer (born 1955) is an American ufologist and a retired physician. He founded the Center for the Study of Extraterrestrial Intelligence (CSETI) and the Disclosure Project, which claims to seek the disclosure of alleged classified UFO information.

sources.quote: Episode title: Meet The Startup Summoning UFOs: Skywatcher Interview

nodes.label: James Valor
nodes.summary: Retired US Army sergeant major (23 years government service, 2020 retirement); lead operations of Skywatcher UAP detection startup; worked with Jake Barber and Ross Coulthart.

sources.quote: Skywatcher lead operations; retired USAF sergeant major

nodes.label: Mr. K (Skywatcher physicist)
nodes.summary: Anonymous Skywatcher co-pioneer; physicist who builds jet engines, flamethrowers, and hot rods; first discovered the UAPs over their 2021 war-game location and the 'dog whistle' mechanism.

sources.quote: Skywatcher pioneer; physicist working on jet engines and hot rods

nodes.label: Mr. D (Skywatcher signals)
nodes.summary: Anonymous Skywatcher co-pioneer; discovered how to detect UAP craft signals via direction-finding and cueing optical cameras to locate them.

sources.quote: Skywatcher pioneer; signals-detection discovery

nodes.label: UAP Dog Whistle
nodes.summary: Skywatcher's central technology: a signal emission that reliably attracts UAP presence at their location; 'after we turn on the dog whistle, UAPs literally come to us day after day.' Origin discovered by Skywatcher co-pioneer Mr. K.

sources.timestamp_end: Skywatcher technology that reliably summons UAPs

nodes.label: Skywatcher UAP Taxonomy (9 Classes)
nodes.summary: Skywatcher's proposed 9-class UAP taxonomy spanning characteristics like shimmer, tumble, spin, apparent-alive-flight, black-body, vapor-sheath, and metallic-glinty morphologies.

nodes.label: Class-1 Tetrahedron UAP
nodes.summary: Skywatcher class-1 UAP: tumbling/spinning high-speed black-bodied tetrahedron wearing a low/high-pressure vapor sheath that reflects sunlight. First sighting was a formation of seven during a 2021 government war-game.

sources.timestamp_end: Skywatcher's first-documented UAP class; tumbling black-body tetrahedron

nodes.label: Skywatcher's UAP discovery occurred by accident during a 2021 DoD-contracted war-game event; a forma
nodes.summary: Skywatcher's UAP discovery occurred by accident during a 2021 DoD-contracted war-game event; a formation of 7 Class-1 tetrahedral UAPs was observed over the blue/red-team exercise.

sources.quote: At one of those war game events in 2021, we had an observation. It turned out to be class one UAPs. We originally thought that was a government made, US government made tool.

type_specific.statement_text: Skywatcher's UAP discovery occurred by accident during a 2021 DoD-contracted war-game event; a formation of 7 Class-1 tetrahedral UAPs was observed over the blue/red-team exercise.

nodes.label: Skywatcher's 'dog whistle' technology reliably summons UAPs at their location day after day; their s
nodes.summary: Skywatcher's 'dog whistle' technology reliably summons UAPs at their location day after day; their sonic-lead Jordan predicts the class and conflict of next-day sightings.

sources.quote: After we turn on the dog whistle, UAPs literally come to us day after day. Jordan our sonic lead came up to me like, tomorrow we're going to see a conflict in the sky.

type_specific.statement_text: Skywatcher's 'dog whistle' technology reliably summons UAPs at their location day after day; their sonic-lead Jordan predicts the class and conflict of next-day sightings.

nodes.label: Skywatcher observed a Class-7 UAP with the naked eye hovering directly overhead — 'very glinty, look
nodes.summary: Skywatcher observed a Class-7 UAP with the naked eye hovering directly overhead — 'very glinty, looked metallic', purple or black through optics, with chrome-like reflections.

sources.quote: We had a class 7 UAP. Before the helo got here it came and hovered right over us. For the first time we saw a class 7 with a naked eye. It was very glinty, looked metallic.

type_specific.statement_text: Skywatcher observed a Class-7 UAP with the naked eye hovering directly overhead — 'very glinty, looked metallic', purple or black through optics, with chrome-like reflections.

nodes.label: A Skywatcher helicopter pursuing a Class-7 UAP was struck by an 'energy weapon' from the UAP mid-tur
nodes.summary: A Skywatcher helicopter pursuing a Class-7 UAP was struck by an 'energy weapon' from the UAP mid-turn before the helicopter could orient toward it.

sources.quote: Mid-turn before he could actually turn it to the UAP, the UAP hit us with an energy weapon.

type_specific.statement_text: A Skywatcher helicopter pursuing a Class-7 UAP was struck by an 'energy weapon' from the UAP mid-turn before the helicopter could orient toward it.

nodes.label: Skywatcher's origin and operations overlap with Jake Barber's retrieval program; Coulthart publicly
nodes.summary: Skywatcher's origin and operations overlap with Jake Barber's retrieval program; Coulthart publicly disclosed Skywatcher's existence in his Barber interview.

sources.quote: A mutual friend Jake Barber who gave me the honor of helping tell his story after Ross Coulthart came out as the first person to retrieve this craft of non-human origin. He mentioned he had this private enterprise he was working on called Skywatcher.

type_specific.statement_text: Skywatcher's origin and operations overlap with Jake Barber's retrieval program; Coulthart publicly disclosed Skywatcher's existence in his Barber interview.

nodes.label: Skywatcher confirms UAPs are emitting in narrow spectral bands that electro-optical sensors cannot s
nodes.summary: Skywatcher confirms UAPs are emitting in narrow spectral bands that electro-optical sensors cannot see natively but cued cameras can — explaining 'UAPs visible on radar but invisible to naked eye.'

sources.quote: That little bit of extra is where they're actually emitting light. We have three spectrums and we see them on radar. We're sitting there with binoculars and can't see them.

type_specific.statement_text: Skywatcher confirms UAPs are emitting in narrow spectral bands that electro-optical sensors cannot see natively but cued cameras can — explaining 'UAPs visible on radar but invisible to naked eye.'

Episode: Meet The Startup Summoning UFOs: Skywatcher Interview
Skywatcher's UAP discovery occurred by accident during a 2021 DoD-contracted war-game event; a formation of 7 Class-1 tetrahedral UAPs was observed over the blue/red-team exercise.
Skywatcher's 'dog whistle' technology reliably summons UAPs at their location day after day; their sonic-lead Jordan predicts the class and conflict of next-day sightings.
Skywatcher observed a Class-7 UAP with the naked eye hovering directly overhead — 'very glinty, looked metallic', purple or black through optics, with chrome-like reflections.
A Skywatcher helicopter pursuing a Class-7 UAP was struck by an 'energy weapon' from the UAP mid-turn before the helicopter could orient toward it.
Skywatcher's origin and operations overlap with Jake Barber's retrieval program; Coulthart publicly disclosed Skywatcher's existence in his Barber interview.
Skywatcher confirms UAPs are emitting in narrow spectral bands that electro-optical sensors cannot see natively but cued cameras can — explaining 'UAPs visible on radar but invisible to naked eye.'
